zh.Mater, Oudenaarde, Flandre-Orientale, Be: 比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德馬特
zh.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE是Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE"和Sybille DE LILLE的兒子，於1250年出生。他和Guillemette de WAVRIN至少有1個女兒。他去世於1302年4月6日在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德馬特享年52歲。

zh-hans.Mater, Oudenaarde, Flandre-Orientale, Be: 比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德馬特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE是Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE"和Sybille DE LILLE的儿子，于1250年出生。他和Guillemette de WAVRIN至少有1个女儿。他去世于1302年4月6日在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德馬特享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: GAVRE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Cecile, Chantal, Francoise, Jacques, Julien, Laurent.
French: occupational name for a blacksmith or ironworker, from a regional form of Old French fevre (see Lefevre ). Compare Farve and Faver .

variants.name: Faver
meanings: Altered form of French Favre ‘blacksmith’ or of its cognate Lefèvre (see Lefevre ). Compare Favor .
English: probably in most cases an altered form of   Feaver  , partly because between the 16th and the 18th centuries its pronunciation was sometimes identical with the word favour (see 4 below).
English: occasionally perhaps also a nickname from Middle English (Old French) favo(u)r, Middle English faver ‘help, beauty, grace, benevolence’.

variants.name: Lefevre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Andre, Emile, Jacques, Joffre, Luc, Adrien, Antoine, Armand, Camille, Eunide, Francois, Germaine.

French and Walloon (Lefèvre): occupational name for a blacksmith or ironworker, Old French fevre, with fused masculine definite article le. One of the most common surnames in France from an early date, coined before the French word for smith, fèvre (from Latin faber ‘maker’), was replaced by forgeron. This surname was also brought to England (in the 16th century by Protestant Walloons and later reinforced by Huguenots from France). It is also established in the Flemish part of Belgium and in Haiti. See also Lefebvre , compare Faver , Lafaver , Lafever , Lefever , and Leffew .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Le Fèvre) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America (in the form Le Fevre).

variants.name: Favret
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Andre, Chantel, Raoul.
French: diminutive of   Favre   ‘blacksmith’.

variants.name: Farve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Cecile.
Altered form of French   Favre   ‘blacksmith’.

nameMeanings.name: ''VAN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: GAVERE"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Michel.
Altered form of French   Lariviere  .

variants.name: Lariviere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Normand, Fernand, Marcel, Adelard, Andre, Gaston, Philibert, Aime, Alcide, Alphonse, Antoine.
French (Larivière):
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Larivière or La Rivière, from Old French riviere ‘river, shore’.

nameMeanings.name: DIT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  African/Muslim Abdoulaye, Mamadou, Ibrahima, Oumar, Ahmad, Aliou, Aminata, Basem, Cheikh, Djibril, Husam, Maan, Mouhamadou, Saliou, Sekou, Serigne, Nabil, Rachid.

West African (Senegal, Mali, Mauritania, Guinea, and Ivory Coast): from the name of the Dia clan of the Fulani people, which may be of the same origin as the name of the town of Dia in Mali and as the name of the medieval Dia Dynasty of Gao, also in Mali. This surname is also found among the Soninke and Tukulor peoples. Compare   Diao   and   Jah  .
Muslim: from a personal name based on Arabic ḍiyā’ ‘light, glow, illumination’ (see Zia ). It is found e.g. in Iraq.

variants.name: Jah
meanings: West African (mainly Sierra Leone and the Gambia): variant of the Fulani name   Dia  .

variants.name: Diao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 刁, meaning ‘trick’ in Chinese: (i) said to be adopted in place of the homophonous character Diao (雕), the name of a state during the Western Zhou dynasty(1046–771  BC ). (ii) from the second element of the personal name Shu Diao (豎刁), personal name of a famous official during the reign of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ); or said to be borne by descendants of Diao Bo (貂勃, also written as 刁勃), an official and a hermit who lived in the state of Qi during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ), who was said to be also a descendent of Shu Diao (豎刁).(iii) also adopted in place of other surnames by some minority ethnic groups from Southwestern China.
West African (Senegal and Burkina Faso): name of Fulani origin, which corresponds to   Dia  .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.

Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

variants.name: Jeane
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jean .

en.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE was born in 1250 as the son of Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE" and Sybille DE LILLE. He had at least 1 daughter with Guillemette de WAVRIN. He died on 6 April 1302, in Mater, Oudenaarde, East Flanders, Belgium, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE est né en 1250, fils de Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE" et Sybille DE LILLE. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Guillemette de WAVRIN. Il est décédé le 6 avril 1302, à Mater, Audenarde, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE wurde 1250, als Sohn von Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE" und Sybille DE LILLE geboren. Er hatte mit Guillemette de WAVRIN mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 6. April 1302 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Mater, Oudenaarde, Ostflandern, Belgien.

it.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE è nato il nel 1250 come figlio di Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE" e Sybille DE LILLE. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Guillemette de WAVRIN. Morì il 6 aprile 1302, nel Mater, Oudenaarde, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.Mater, Oudenaarde, Flandre-Orientale, Be: ベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンオウデナアルドマター
ja.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSEは1250年にRasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE"とSybille DE LILLEの息子として生まれた。彼はGuillemette de WAVRINとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1302年4月6日にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンオウデナアルドマターで52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE(은)는 1250년 Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE"(와)과 Sybille DE LILLE의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Guillemette de WAVRIN(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1302년 4월 6일 벨기에 스트-블라아더 우데나아르드 마터에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE nasceu em 1250 filho de Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE" e Sybille DE LILLE. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Guillemette de WAVRIN. Ele faleceu em 6 de abril de 1302, em Mater, Oudenaarde, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE родился в 1250 г. Его родителей звали Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE" и Sybille DE LILLE. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Guillemette de WAVRIN. Он умер 6 апреля 1302 г. в местности мэитир, оуденаард, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jean I DE GAVRE ''VAN GAVERE" DIT van SCHORISSE nació en 1250 como hijo de Rasse Jean Dit L'Illustre DE GAVRE "VAN GAVERE" y Sybille DE LILLE. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Guillemette de WAVRIN. Murió el 6 de abril de 1302, en Mater, Oudenaarde, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, a la edad de 52 años.
